author: @MrTrevorkemp
text: It would have been nice if this demonstration had been an actual demonstration. It doesn't come through very clearly at all and unless somebody who has 20/20 vision is watching this video, they probably didn't notice just like for me because the room you're in is so well lit. You cannot tell whether or not the rack of light bulbs that you have there to help demonstrate this as a flywheel generator are actually lit up or not. It would be nice next time if you're going to use lights to demonstrate whether a device that makes electricity works or not would actually be something we could see. Maybe dim the lights in your workshop or use a light that is much brighter and much more contrasting to the lights in your workshop and the background in your workshop because this one just demonstrates that the weights helped to keep it spinning for a decent chunk of time. It did not necessarily demonstrate that you were generating any reasonable amount of power to even light up those lights, because again, your workshop is so brightly lit and you're using lights that aren't that bright in the first place to demonstrate something. Maybe next time you try this demonstration, you should turn the workshop lighting down. Have it really dim so that way yes, it comes through that you're pulling the flywheel generator to a start and then all the sudden those lights come on and are noticeable and bright on the screen instead of leaving us the viewers asking, are those lights actually lit or are they just reacting like those lights are wet which is what it was for me and my experience on this particular video. Not a good demonstration of showing this as a flywheel generator effectively producing electricity, and I do feel that maybe you could have used a little thicker string or even a longer string and maybe pulled a little bit harder to build up even more RPMs than you built up in this particular demonstration. You did successfully demonstrate that the flywheel effect will cause this thing to go for a few minutes on a single pole. But you did fail in this video to demonstrate that it was actually spinning fast enough to make those lights light up because the lights that you use to light the workshop. We're just way too bright and those lights that you were using for the demonstration could not contrast that super bright light and obviously be glowing under the power of that generator.

author: @magapefarmshomestead6453
text: Is the serpentine coil considered symmetrical or asymmetrical. Most coils, for a long time, have been constructed symmetrically, which is not the most favorable nor best design. Asymmetrical designs infer lower reaction to other disruptive and undesirable reactive problems like lens law etc.

author: @sinenomine9093
text: Just a comment about flywheel generators, not far from me there was a larger older coal-fired generation station with five big steam turbine generator sets.  A while ago three of the turbines were converted to natural gas and two were partially decommissioned.  The two generators were retained as flywheel storage to manage the supply vs demand instead of trying to follow the demand by regulating the steam supply closely.   When the demand declines the excess power is diverted to the two freewheeling generators to increase rpm.  Then if the demand suddenly returns, the power stored in the spinning generators is used for immediate supply.

author: @jayhuyssoon9486
text:
Next try is to put this machine together with your flat wind turbine.  I think that two things will happen, one is the flywheel will generate a more constant speed for a longer period, two the flywheel will keep the the mechanism from running away in higher velocity winds.  I also thought if you include your magnetically enhanced system, your machine would be self powered for a longer period of time.  

For me, living in one of the five windiest spots in the world, this might give me some power day or night.   You have gotten my creative thought juices going.   I enjoy you videos immensely, some day I would like to start working on some of your ideas and mind.  Thanks again.

The fact that it spun for so long is a sign that you were not extracting much energy out of the system.

Is there a way to push the flywheel when force is enacted on the feathers and release when there is no wind or the flywheel is spinning faster than the feather rotor. Seems like that would be a great way to get many times more work out of wind's energy..  The compactness/density of these latest projects is amazing. A decent little power source in the form factor of a large coffee can. Great work sir.

author: @rowgler1
text: This video reminded me of the 'living energy machine", one of the first videos I ever saw on you-tube. It's still up with only 84 subscribers and 12 comments. There was a design I saw in a book in the 80s where ropes were attached to the tops of several tall trees. They were routed through pulleys to a flywheel. When the trees would move in the wind the ropes would pull and turn the ratcheted flywheel. If those two designs were combined with your serpentine rotor that would be quite a thing.🌬🌴🌪⚡

replies.author: @Sedgewise47
replies.text: 🤔Can you recall the title or author of the book?…

replies.author: @rowgler1
replies.text: @Sedgewise47 I'm sorry, I don't remember. It was published in the 70's, tall and thin. I got it from a library, don't have a copy. Maybe someone else can help. I remember the working part was in the center of the trees it was hooked up to. When any of the six or seven trees swayed away from it the pulling of the ropes did work. Intermittent motion from several sources converted to rotary motion. It might have been designed to pump water. It was so pre-computer, my searches on the subject haven't worked. Some other old guy might know?... The ropes didn't move very far, only a few feet, but they had considerable force.

author: @alexwild4350
text: But isn't this a less than zero sum game ? The more power drawn out of the coils for voltage generation, the bigger the back emf from the coils resisting the generation. text:
I have a big ford truck flywheel  i want to do this with. 

I went to tinkercad and searched for your name,  and Darwin Vent and found nothing.

author: @dannywilson5852
text: Could use that with a gravity weight and extra cogs run for ages

author: @krkfire-976
text: Robert, could you reduce the friction using magnetic bearings?  Thrust bearings are good but if you can levitate the rotor and even center it using magnets it might perform better.  Just a thought.

replies.author: @DavyOneness
replies.text: Can't  levitate AND have a flywheel this heavy at sametime. If you use too strong of magnets it is going to mess with the fields you are trying to use

replies.author: @ogi22
replies.text: @DavyOneness That's exactly what i thought. Any metal flywheel within the proximity to magnets strong enough to lift it, would create eddy currents.

replies.author: @bikerfirefarter7280
replies.text: @DavyOneness yes you can. The levitation fields are not 'cut' by the windings nor do they create eddy currents. Try it with a couple of old microwave or speaker magnets, the only issue is keeping them centralised. Anyway the fields can easily be shielded.

author: @toml.8210
text:
You could've had a fag while watching it go?
OTOH-  it would be usefulkfor places whjere the wind is not consistant.

author: @jimdaly5077
text: Thank you , 👍🐝🌞

author: @KavanOBrien
text: I was waiting for you to pull the other one , do you ever think you will come up with a system that one could use as a form of generating energy to store in a battery, be it these small items  or a combination of small items to store into battery storage.

replies.author: @docink6175
replies.text: I think many of the ideas he's shown would work to charge a battery even if its just a small amount and using a combination of vibration generation, a wind powered device and water powered generation you could harvest small amounts of energy from multiple sources... Imagine this snake generator for example with the flat windvanes he made a few vdo's ago, a dozen of those on your roof would be unobtrusive or the generator he made from a long fan blade could sit on the gutter edge and ridge of the house to harvest any slight breeze

replies.author: @KavanOBrien
replies.text: @docink6175 I would not which battery that could take the charge , I live on my own and was thinking about buying a battery that could take the storage from several items at the same time and with the combination of all the items would be better than solar and a lot cheaper.

replies.author: @docink6175
replies.text: @KavanOBrien cheap cheap ios any old car starter battery, better but still rather inexpensive is a deep cycle marine battery. I have one in both my trucks and charge them thru the vehicle or a small 40w panel, Im looking at a used 100w panel for $80

replies.author: @KavanOBrien
replies.text: @docink6175 I was thinking more along the lines of a power wall battery set up but to build it so I could generate enough energy through all the small items put it into the battery that would take me off grid.

author: @susannaurand4470
text: Fascinating!!! Love it!!! Great job as always!!! Thank you for sharing!!!

author: @Edmorbus
text: Thanks for sharing

author: @alwardslab8732
text: you can put the flywheel on the center and double the gears and generator each side, and make it a gravity battery with some other weight, nice

replies.author: @markusgarvey
replies.text: A larger diameter flywheel work better as well.

author: @AtlasReburdened
text: Tsk tsk tsk

author: @industrialmonk
text: Hi did you notice when you added the extra bearing the reduction of vertical movement in the rotor & another great improvement 👍

author: @davidpeckham2405
text: 2nd  Great vid.  Makes me want to convert my dead pull-start lawn mower into a genny for emergency purposes.  Thx from Ontario, Canada
